sections.paragraph:
Ῥουβήν, ὁ indecl. (רְאוּבֵן; LXX, Philo. Ῥουβήμ Test12Patr [in part]; JosAs 27:6. Ῥουβίμ Test12Patr [in part] and Just., D. 120, 1. Ῥουβίν Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 3 al.; Joseph. has Ῥουβῆλος, ου [Ant. 1, 307]) Reuben, oldest son of Jacob and Leah (Gen 29:32) Rv 7:5. Ῥουβήλ (v.l. Ῥουβίμ) GJs 1:2; 6:3.—BHHW III 1624.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 907
